Result: (1) 描述了GenAI在软件工程生命周期中的实践特征，区分了新兴工作流；(2) 学生推荐了具体用例并提出了强调验证和保持独立理解的责任使用指南；(3) 客户强烈支持GenAI使用，但对理解、质量和数据保护有明确期望；(4) 提出未来课程需制定明确的责任使用指南、定向AI素养资源和团队级治理角色。

Conclusion: 该研究为生成式AI时代基于证据的教学干预提供了现状基线，强调了在RWCPs中平衡GenAI赋能与学习目标、建立负责任使用框架的必要性。

Abstract: Real-world Capstone Projects (RWCPs) are a key component of software engineering education, enabling students to develop software for external clients under authentic conditions. Their high ecological validity, combined with substantial variation in domains, technologies, and stakeholders, typically requires flexible and minimally prescriptive teaching approaches. The rapid integration of generative AI (GenAI) into professional software development adds new challenges: students are expected to use AI tools that are common in practice, yet unguided use may affect learning, collaboration, and consistency in ways that are not yet well understood.
  To establish an empirical baseline for responsible GenAI integration, we conducted a large-scale study of self-determined GenAI use in an undergraduate RWCP course. The module involved 178 students working in 18 teams across 15 client projects over four months, with GenAI use explicitly permitted. We collected mixed-method survey data from 150 students on attitudes, usage prevalence, workflows, use cases, and perceived benefits and risks, and surveyed client stakeholders regarding expectations and concerns.
  Our findings provide (1) a characterization of GenAI practices across the software engineering lifecycle, including a distinction between emerging workflows; (2) student-recommended use cases and responsible-use directives emphasizing verification and maintaining independent understanding; (3) client perspectives highlighting strong support for GenAI use but clear expectations regarding understanding, quality, and data protection; and (4) implications for future course iterations, including the need for explicit responsible-use guidelines, targeted AI literacy resources, and team-level governance roles. This study offers a status quo baseline for evidence-based pedagogical interventions in the era of GenAI.

</details>


### [472] [Understanding the Limits of Automated Evaluation for Code Review Bots in Practice](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.24525)
*Veli Karakaya,Utku Boran Torun,Baykal Mehmet Uçar,Eray Tüzün*

Main category: cs.SE

TL;DR: 本文研究了工业环境下自动化评估LLM驱动的代码审查机器人评论的可行性，发现自动化评估方法（G-Eval和LLM-as-a-Judge）与开发人员标注仅达到中等一致性（0.44-0.62），原因在于开发者行为受工作流压力和组织因素影响，无法作为客观真实标签。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 随着ACR机器人被广泛采用，如何可靠且规模化地评估机器人生成的评论是否有用成为关键挑战。实践中依赖开发者动作和标注作为评估依据，但这些信号受上下文和组织因素影响，其客观性存疑。

Method: 使用Beko公司的工业数据集（2,604条PR评论，标注为fixed/wontFix），应用G-Eval和LLM-as-a-Judge两种自动化评估方法，采用二元决策和0-4 Likert量表两种形式，对比Gemini-2.5-pro、GPT-4.1-mini、GPT-5.2三个模型的表现。

Result: 自动化评估与人工标注的一致性比值约在0.44到0.62之间，不同模型和评估设计（二元vs Likert量表）下结果存在显著差异。开发者行为（如解决或忽略评论）不仅反映评论质量，还受上下文约束、优先级决策和工作流动态影响。

Conclusion: 完全自动化评估工业环境中ACR机器人评论存在实际局限性。开发者标注行为受工作流压力和组织约束强烈影响，不宜将其视为客观真实标签，自动化评估需谨慎设计。

Abstract: Automated code review (ACR) bots are increasingly used in industrial software development to assist developers during pull request (PR) review. As adoption grows, a key challenge is how to evaluate the usefulness of bot-generated comments reliably and at scale. In practice, such evaluation often relies on developer actions and annotations that are shaped by contextual and organizational factors, complicating their use as objective ground truth. We examine the feasibility and limitations of automating the evaluation of LLM-powered ACR bots in an industrial setting. We analyze an industrial dataset from Beko comprising 2,604 bot-generated PR comments, each labeled by software engineers as fixed/wontFix. Two automated evaluation approaches, G-Eval and an LLM-as-a-Judge pipeline, are applied using both binary decisions and a 0-4 Likert-scale formulation, enabling a controlled comparison against developer-provided labels. Across Gemini-2.5-pro, GPT-4.1-mini, and GPT-5.2, both evaluation strategies achieve only moderate alignment with human labels. Agreement ratios range from approximately 0.44 to 0.62, with noticeable variation across models and between binary and Likert-scale formulations, indicating sensitivity to both model choice and evaluation design. Our findings highlight practical limitations in fully automating the evaluation of ACR bot comments in industrial contexts. Developer actions such as resolving or ignoring comments reflect not only comment quality, but also contextual constraints, prioritization decisions, and workflow dynamics that are difficult to capture through static artifacts. Insights from a follow-up interview with a software engineering director further corroborate that developer labeling behavior is strongly influenced by workflow pressures and organizational constraints, reinforcing the challenges of treating such signals as objective ground truth.

</details>


### [473] [Mono2Sls: Automated Monolith-to-Serverless Migration via Multi-Stage Pipeline with Static Analysis](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.24550)
*Xingyan Chen,Yuxin Su,Zishan Su,Yang Yu,Zibin Zheng*

Main category: cs.SE

TL;DR: 本文提出Mono2Sls自动化管线，将单体Web后端转换为可部署的AWS SAM应用，结合静态分析和四个LLM智能体，在六个基准应用上实现100%部署成功率和66.1%端到端正确性。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 将现有单体后端迁移到云平台仍然是一项困难的软件工程任务，需要协调修改程序结构、源码、基础设施配置和云特定设计决策，且大部分工作仍手工完成。

Method: 提出Mono2Sls自动化管线，结合轻量级静态分析（入口点、调用图、异步行为）与四个顺序执行的LLM智能体（架构师、代码开发者、SAM工程师、一致性验证器），智能体通过中间工件通信并查阅SAM知识库。

Result: 在六个基准应用（10K+行代码、76个业务端点）上，部署成功率100%，端到端正确性66.1%，API覆盖F1分数98.7%；商业基线分别为53.7-61.2%和88.4%。消融实验表明静态分析引导的架构规划贡献23.4个百分点。

Conclusion: Mono2Sls能有效自动化单体后端到无服务器架构的迁移，显著优于商业基线，静态分析引导的架构规划是关键贡献因素。

Abstract: Cloud computing platforms offer elastic scaling, managed infrastructure, and pay-per-use pricing, but moving existing monolithic backends to them remains a difficult software engineering task. In practice, the migration requires coordinated changes to program structure, source code, infrastructure configuration, and cloud-specific design decisions, and these changes are still largely carried out by hand. In this paper, we present Mono2Sls, an automated pipeline that converts monolithic web backends into deployable AWS SAM applications. The pipeline combines lightweight static analysis of entry points, call graphs, and asynchronous behavior with four sequential tool-using LLM agents: Architect, Code Developer, SAM Engineer, and Consistency Validator. These agents communicate through explicit intermediate artifacts and consult a curated SAM knowledge base. Evaluated on six benchmark applications totaling more than 10K lines of code and 76 business endpoints, Mono2Sls achieves 100% deployment success without manual fixes. It also reaches 66.1% end-to-end correctness and 98.7% API-coverage F1, whereas the commercial baselines achieve 53.7--61.2% and 88.4%, respectively. The migrated systems show more consistent use of AWS-native authentication and asynchronous patterns, and an ablation study indicates that static-analysis-guided architecture planning contributes 23.4 percentage points to end-to-end correctness.

</details>


### [474] [Measuring the Unmeasurable: Markov Chain Reliability for LLM Agents](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.24579)
*Phat T. Tran-Truong,Xuan-Bach Le*

Main category: cs.SE

TL;DR: 本文提出TraceToChain，一个将LLM代理执行轨迹拟合成吸收态离散时间马尔可夫链（DTMC）的可复现pipeline，通过显式诊断和不确定性量化来评估代理可靠性，统一了pass@k、pass^k和RDC等指标。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 现有LLM代理可靠性评估依赖标量基准指标（如pass@k、pass^k、RDC），但这些指标未明确估计成功-时间分布、未检验迹是否支持该分布，也未量化有限迹的不确定性。

Method: 构建自动聚类分类法，使用拉普拉斯平滑MLE估计转移概率，通过复合AIC和KS拟合优度检验检查拟合质量，报告Dirichlet后验可信区间和非参数bootstrap区间，将经典可靠性数学（Kemeny-Snell、Cheung、Goel-Okumoto）适配到代理迹分析中。

Result: 在7个MAST风格框架上采用严格50/50拟合/测试协议，保留的实证RDC与解析RDC最大L∞为0.053（中位数0.048）；双样本KS检验在7/7框架上接受拟合链（最小p=0.78）；每个条目的95%后验和bootstrap区间在中位数处一致至≈0.01。

Conclusion: TraceToChain能够将以往分开报告的指标统一为单一成功-时间分布，提供良好的拟合效果和可靠的不确定性量化，为评估LLM代理系统可靠性提供了更严谨的统计框架。

Abstract: Large language model (LLM) agents increasingly operate as sequential software systems, but their reliability is often summarized by scalar benchmark metrics. Metrics such as pass$@k$, pass$^k$, and the reliability decay curve (RDC) are useful summaries, but they do not identify the success-time distribution being estimated, test whether traces support that distribution, or quantify finite-trace uncertainty. We present \textsc{TraceToChain}, a reproducible pipeline that fits agent execution traces to an absorbing discrete-time Markov chain (DTMC), $\hat M=(\hat Q,\hat R_\oplus,\hat R_\ominus)$, with explicit diagnostics and uncertainty. The pipeline builds an automatic cluster taxonomy, estimates transitions with Laplace-smoothed maximum-likelihood estimation (MLE), checks fit with a composite Akaike information criterion (AIC) and Kolmogorov--Smirnov (KS) goodness-of-fit certificate, and reports Dirichlet-posterior credible intervals and non-parametric bootstrap intervals. We adapt classical reliability mathematics (Kemeny--Snell~\cite{kemenysnell}, Cheung~\cite{cheung1980}, Goel--Okumoto~\cite{goelokt}) to agent traces. The resulting first-passage view reconciles metrics usually reported separately: pass$@k$, pass$^k$, and the RDC are projections of one success-time distribution. On seven controlled MAST-style frameworks with a strict 50/50 fit/test protocol, held-out empirical RDCs overlay their analytic counterparts with max $L_\infty^{\mathrm{RDC}} = 0.053$ (median $0.048$). A two-sample KS test on the first-passage cumulative distribution function (CDF) accepts the fitted chain with $p>0.05$ on $7/7$ frameworks (min $p = 0.78$), and per-entry $95\%$ posterior and bootstrap intervals agree to $\approx\!0.01$ at the median.

</details>


### [475] [Evaluation of LLM-Based Software Engineering Tools: Practices, Challenges, and Future Directions](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.24621)
*Utku Boran Torun,Veli Karakaya,Ali Babar,Eray Tüzün*

Main category: cs.SE

TL;DR: 本文系统探讨了基于大语言模型的软件工程工具在评估中面临的五大核心挑战（缺乏稳定基准真相、主观性与多维度质量、非确定性导致的评估不稳定、自动/模型评估局限、实践碎片化），并提出了未来发展方向。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 随着LLM驱动的AI4SE工具从原型走向大规模部署，传统软件工程和机器学习评估假设（单一基准真相、确定性输出、客观正确性）被打破，亟需重新审视"可靠评估"的定义与方法。

Method: 通过综述视角，将LLM评估作为通用的、任务依赖的概念，以SE任务为具体场景，分析现有评估实践、局限性及挑战，并归纳未来研究方向。

Result: 识别出五类关键挑战：1) 缺乏稳定基准真相；2) 主观性与多维质量；3) 非确定性导致评估不稳定；4) 自动与模型评估的局限；5) 评估实践碎片化。

Conclusion: 需要构建更鲁棒、可扩展、可信的评估方法论，以跟上LLM在SE领域日益增长的角色。

Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly embedded in software engineering (SE) tools, powering applications such as code generation, automated code review, and bug triage. As these LLM-based AI for Software Engineering (AI4SE) systems transition from experimental prototypes to widely deployed tools, the question of what it means to evaluate their behavior reliably has become both critical and unanswered. Unlike traditional SE or machine learning systems, LLM-based tools often produce open-ended, natural language outputs, admit multiple valid answers, and exhibit non-deterministic behavior across runs. These characteristics fundamentally challenge long-standing evaluation assumptions such as the existence of a single ground truth, deterministic outputs, and objective correctness. In this paper, we examine LLM evaluation as a general, task-dependent concept through the lens of SE tasks. We discuss why reliable evaluation is essential for trust, adoption, and meaningful assessment of LLM-based tools, summarize the current state of evaluation practices, and highlight their limitations in realistic AI4SE settings. We then identify key challenges facing current approaches, including the absence of stable ground truth, subjectivity and multi-dimensional quality, evaluation instability due to non-determinism, limitations of automated and model-based evaluation, and fragmentation of evaluation practices. Finally, we outline future directions aimed at advancing LLM evaluation toward more robust, scalable, and trustworthy methodologies, to stimulate discussion on principled evaluation practices that can keep pace with the growing role of LLMs in SE.

</details>


### [476] [Less Is More: Engineering Challenges of On-Device Small Language Model Integration in a Mobile Application](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.24636)
*William Oliveira*

Main category: cs.SE

TL;DR: 本文通过一个5天的工程冲刺案例，记录了在安卓猜词游戏Palabrita中集成小语言模型（Gemma 4 E2B 2.6B和Qwen3 0.6B）的实践挑战。系统从LLM生成完整结构化谜题的雄心设计方案，演变为仅生成三条简短提示的务实架构。研究识别出五类故障，并总结了八条设计启发式规则：最可靠的端上LLM功能是让LLM做得最少。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 验证端上小语言模型能否在真实的移动端生产应用中实现完全离线、私密的AI体验，揭示从理论可能性到实际工程落地的差距。

Method: 纵向从业者案例研究，在5天204次提交的冲刺中，对两款SLM（Gemma 4 E2B 2.6B和Qwen3 0.6B）进行集成实验，记录失败分类与缓解策略。

Result: 识别出五类故障：输出格式违规、约束违规、上下文质量退化、延迟不兼容、模型选择不稳定。提出了多层防御解析、上下文重试、会话轮换、渐进式提示硬化、系统性责任减少等缓解策略。

Conclusion: 端上SLM在移动生产应用中是可行的，但开发者必须接受一个根本约束：最可靠的端上LLM功能是让LLM做得最少。文章提炼出八条可操作的设计启发式规则。

Abstract: On-device Small Language Models (SLMs) promise fully offline, private AI experiences for mobile users (no cloud dependency, no data leaving the device). But is this promise achievable in practice? This paper presents a longitudinal practitioner case study documenting the engineering challenges of integrating SLMs (Gemma 4 E2B, 2.6B parameters; Qwen3 0.6B, 600M parameters) into Palabrita, a production Android word-guessing game. Over a 5-day development sprint comprising 204 commits (~90 directly AI-related), the system underwent a radical transformation: from an ambitious design where the LLM generated complete structured puzzles (word, category, difficulty, and five hints as JSON) to a pragmatic architecture where curated word lists provide the words and the LLM generates only three short hints, with a deterministic fallback if it fails. We identify five categories of failures specific to on-device SLM integration: output format violations, constraint violations, context quality degradation, latency incompatibility, and model selection instability. For each failure category, we document the observed symptoms, root causes, and the prompt engineering and architectural strategies that effectively mitigated them, including multi-layer defensive parsing, contextual retry with failure feedback, session rotation, progressive prompt hardening, and systematic responsibility reduction. Our findings demonstrate that on-device SLMs are viable for production mobile applications, but only when the developer accepts a fundamental constraint: the most reliable on-device LLM feature is one where the LLM does the least. We distill our experience into eight actionable design heuristics for practitioners integrating SLMs into mobile apps.

</details>


### [477] [Leveraging LLMs for Multi-File DSL Code Generation: An Industrial Case Study](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.24678)
*Sivajeet Chand,Kevin Nguyen,Peter Kuntz,Alexander Pretschner*

Main category: cs.SE

TL;DR: 宝马工业案例研究，探索将代码LLM适配到Xtext领域特定语言(DSL)，实现从单一自然语言指令生成多文件仓库级DSL代码，通过QLoRA微调在7B模型上取得最优效果。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: LLM在通用代码生成上表现优异，但面向企业领域特定语言(DSL)的仓库级多文件变更生成尚未被充分探索，特别是从单条自然语言指令生成跨文件夹、多文件的DSL工件。

Method: 构建端到端流水线：设计路径保留的JSON结构编码DSL文件夹层级，支持单次响应生成仓库规模代码；对比三种配置（基线提示、单样本上下文学习、QLoRA参数高效微调），评估两个7B指令调优代码LLM（Qwen2.5-Coder和DeepSeek-Coder）。

Result: 微调在所有模型和指标上取得最大提升，多文件输出上精确匹配准确率高、编辑相似度大、结构保真度达1.00；单样本上下文学习提升较小但稳定；通过专家调查和代码生成器执行验证了实际效用。

Conclusion: 参数高效微调(QLoRA)是适配LLM到企业DSL多文件生成的最有效策略，结构保真编码是学习跨文件依赖的关键设计。

Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) perform strongly on general-purpose code generation, yet their applicability to enterprise domain-specific languages (DSLs) remains underexplored, especially for repository-scale change generation spanning multiple files and folder structures from a single natural-language (NL) instruction. We report an industrial case study at BMW that adapts code-oriented LLMs to generate and modify project-root DSL artifacts for an Xtext-based DSL that drives downstream Java/TypeScript code generation. We develop an end-to-end pipeline for dataset construction, multi-file task representation, model adaptation, and evaluation. We encode DSL folder hierarchies as structured, path-preserving JSON, allowing single-response generation at repository scale and learning cross-file dependencies. We evaluate two instruction-tuned code LLMs (Qwen2.5-Coder and DeepSeek-Coder, 7B) under three configurations: baseline prompting, one-shot in-context learning, and parameter-efficient fine-tuning (QLoRA). Beyond standard similarity metrics, we introduce task-specific measures that assess edit correctness and repository structural fidelity. Fine-tuning yields the most significant gains across models and metrics, achieving high exact-match accuracy, substantial edit similarity, and structural fidelity of 1.00 on our held-out set for multi-file outputs. At the same time, one-shot in-context learning provides smaller but consistent improvements over baseline prompting. We further validate practical utility via an expert developer survey and an execution-based check using the existing code generator.

</details>


### [478] [Defective Task Descriptions in LLM-Based Code Generation: Detection and Analysis](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.24703)
*Amal Akli,Mike Papadakis,Maxime Cordy,Yves Le Traon*

Main category: cs.SE

TL;DR: 提出SpecValidator，一种基于小模型参数高效微调的轻量级分类器，用于自动检测代码生成任务描述中的缺陷（词汇模糊、欠规范、语法格式），在3个基准上显著优于GPT-5-mini和Claude Sonnet 4，并发现LLM对任务描述缺陷的鲁棒性主要取决于缺陷类型和任务描述特征而非模型容量。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 用户提供的任务描述可能存在缺陷，严重影响LLM代码生成的正确性，而现有方法假设任务描述足够详细和规范。

Method: 基于小模型通过参数高效微调（PEFT）训练轻量级分类器SpecValidator，用于检测三种类型缺陷：词汇模糊、欠规范、语法格式。

Result: SpecValidator在缺陷检测上F1=0.804、MCC=0.745，显著优于GPT-5-mini（F1=0.469）和Claude Sonnet 4（F1=0.518）；能泛化到未见问题和检测真实基准中的未知欠规范缺陷。

Conclusion: LLM对任务描述缺陷的鲁棒性主要取决于缺陷类型和任务描述特征而非模型容量；欠规范缺陷最为严重；上下文更丰富的基准（如LiveCodeBench）更具弹性，强调结构化任务描述对可靠代码生成的重要性。

Abstract: Large language models are widely used for code generation, yet they rely on an implicit assumption that the task descriptions are sufficiently detailed and well-formed. However, in practice, users may provide defective descriptions, which can have a strong effect on code correctness. To address this issue, we develop SpecValidator, a lightweight classifier based on a small model that has been parameter-efficiently finetuned, to automatically detect task description defects. We evaluate SpecValidator on three types of defects, Lexical Vagueness, Under-Specification and Syntax-Formatting on 3 benchmarks with task descriptions of varying structure and complexity. Our results show that SpecValidator achieves defect detection of F1 = 0.804 and MCC = 0.745, significantly outperforming GPT-5-mini (F1 = 0.469 and MCC = 0.281) and Claude Sonnet 4 (F1 = 0.518 and MCC = 0.359). Perhaps more importantly, our analysis indicates that SpecValidator can generalize to unseen issues and detect unknown Under-Specification defects in the original (real) descriptions of the benchmarks used. Our results also show that the robustness of LLMs in task description defects depends primarily on the type of defect and the characteristics of the task description, rather than the capacity of the model, with Under-Specification defects being the most severe. We further found that benchmarks with richer contextual grounding, such as LiveCodeBench, exhibit substantially greater resilience, highlighting the importance of structured task descriptions for reliable LLM-based code generation.

</details>


### [479] [When Prompt Under-Specification Improves Code Correctness: An Exploratory Study of Prompt Wording and Structure Effects on LLM-Based Code Generation](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.24712)
*Amal AKLI,Mike PAPADAKIS,Maxime CORDY,Yves Le TRAON*

Main category: cs.SE

TL;DR: 提示结构、任务复杂度和规格丰富度会影响LLM代码生成对提示变异的鲁棒性；结构丰富的任务描述（如LiveCodeBench）能缓解欠规格带来的负面影响，甚至在特定情况下提升正确率。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 已有研究显示自然语言提示的微小变化（尤其是欠规格）会显著降低代码正确性，但这些结论基于HumanEval等最小规格基准，结构冗余有限，可能夸大了敏感性。本研究旨在探索提示结构、任务复杂度和规格丰富度如何交互影响LLM对提示变异的鲁棒性。

Method: 在HumanEval和结构更丰富的LiveCodeBench上评估10种不同模型，通过对比相同欠规格变异在两个基准上的表现差异，分析提示结构对鲁棒性的影响；同时进行人工分析，识别提示变异提升正确性的机制。

Result: 鲁棒性并非LLM的固定属性，而是高度依赖于提示结构：欠规格变异在HumanEval上会降低性能，但在LiveCodeBench上净效应接近零。在LiveCodeBench中，欠规格有时反而能破坏误导性的词汇或结构线索，从而提升正确性。人工分析识别出三种提升机制：破坏过拟合术语、移除误导性约束、消除虚假标识符触发。

Conclusion: 结构丰富的任务描述能显著缓解欠规格的负面影响，甚至在某些情况下提升正确性。研究归纳了能正面影响LLM代码生成行为的提示修改类别，为编写鲁棒提示提供了实践指导。

Abstract: Large language models are increasingly used for code generation, yet the correctness of their outputs depends not only on model capability but also on how tasks are specified. Prior studies demonstrate that small changes in natural language prompts, particularly under-specification can substantially reduce code correctness; however, these findings are largely based on minimal-specification benchmarks such as HumanEval and MBPP, where limited structural redundancy may exaggerate sensitivity. In this exploratory study, we investigate how prompt structure, task complexity, and specification richness interact with LLM robustness to prompt mutations. We evaluate 10 different models across HumanEval and the structurally richer LiveCodeBench. Our results reveal that robustness is not a fixed property of LLMs but is highly dependent on prompt structure: the same under-specification mutations that degrade performance on HumanEval have near-zero net effect on LiveCodeBench due to redundancy across descriptions, constraints, examples, and I/O conventions. Surprisingly, we also find that prompt mutations can improve correctness. In LiveCodeBench, under-specification often breaks misleading lexical or structural cues that trigger incorrect retrieval-based solution strategies, leading to correctness improvements that counterbalance degradations. Manual analysis identifies consistent mechanisms behind these improvements, including the disruption of over-fitted terminology, removal of misleading constraints, and elimination of spurious identifier triggers. Overall, our study shows that structurally rich task descriptions can substantially mitigate the negative effects of under-specification and, in some cases, even enhance correctness. We outline categories of prompt modifications that positively influence the behavior of LLM code-generation, offering practical insights for writing robust prompts.

</details>
